title: Turkey Meatloaf
description: From Barefoot Contessa Cookbook — Ina's take on the ultimate American comfort food. A generous amount of caramelized onions and garlic give this turkey meatloaf incredible depth of flavor, and a ketchup glaze on top caramelizes into something irresistible. Baking it with a pan of water keeps it perfectly moist.
instructions: In a medium sauté pan, heat the olive oil over medium-low heat. Add the onions and garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, for 8 to 10 minutes until the onions are translucent but not browned. Add the thyme, Worcestershire sauce, chicken stock, and tomato paste. Mix well. Allow to cool to room temperature.
Combine the ground turkey, bread crumbs, eggs, and onion mixture in a large bowl. Mix well and shape into a rectangular loaf on an ungreased baking sheet. Spread the ketchup evenly on top.
Place the meatloaf on the center rack of the oven. Place a pan of hot water on the rack below it. Bake for 1 to 1¼ hours, until the internal temperature is 160°F and the meatloaf is cooked through.
Remove from the oven and allow to rest for 10 minutes. Slice and serve.
tips: The pan of water below the meatloaf is Ina's secret to keeping it moist — don't skip it.
Caramelizing the onions fully before adding them is what gives this meatloaf its extraordinary flavor.
This is even better the next day — slice cold and reheat in the oven, or make sandwiches.
Use 85% lean ground turkey, not 99% fat-free, which will be too dry.
Source: Barefoot Contessa Cookbook by Ina Garten (1999)
